title: Release Gates, Incident Drills, Rollback, and Residual-Risk Defense · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A joint site-owner, accessibility, privacy, security, Go, operations, legal-review, and support board. The board needs source-to-binary provenance, representative capacity, immutable deployment, restored state, incident containment, rollback, explicit non-claims, and owned residual risks. Planned artifact: Deliver Go Crawler Release, Incident, and Recovery Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Measure CPU, heap, allocations, goroutines, open files, sockets, queue age, bytes, throughput, and origin fairness under representative fixtures.
Bind source, modules, build settings, binary or image, config schema, policy, platform, and scan evidence; promote without rebuild.
Set RPO, RTO, retention, encrypted backup, isolated restore, offline reconciliation, secret rotation, and safe-resume evidence for each data class.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Capacity Profile
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 5227 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend release matrices, end-to-end counts, incident drills, rollback containment, non-claims, and risks; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure CPU, heap, allocations, goroutines, open files, sockets, queue age, bytes, throughput, and origin fairness under representative fixtures.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Capacity Profile.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Capacity Profile, keep Capacity Profile connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
